description:
You are an observability engineer who needs to tune an existing dashboard template.  
The template already lives on the system. Your job is to:  

1. Copy the provided template file
   /home/user/observability/templates/metrics_base.json  
   into a **new** file named  
   /home/user/observability/dashboard/dashboard_config.json  
   (create the dashboard/ directory if it does not yet exist).

2. In the new dashboard_config.json, make the following edits ONLY:  
   a. Change the value of the "title" field from  
      Base Metrics Dashboard  
      to  
      Prod API Overview  
   b. Change the value of the "refresh" field from  
      10s  
      to  
      5s  
   c. Append the tag "tuned" to the existing "tags" array so that the array
      becomes ["base", "tuned"].

3. Create a plain-text log file at  
   /home/user/observability/tuning.log  
   that records exactly what you changed.  
   The log must contain **exactly three lines** in this order and follow this
   strict format (no leading/trailing spaces):  

       changed:title:Base Metrics Dashboard->Prod API Overview
       changed:refresh:10s->5s
       added:tag:tuned

   • Use the words “changed” and “added” as shown.  
   • Use the arrow “->” with no surrounding spaces other than the single ones
     shown above.  
   • End each line with a single newline character.

When you are done, the directory /home/user/observability/dashboard should exist
and contain the tuned dashboard_config.json, and the log file
/home/user/observability/tuning.log should contain the three lines above.

Only three types of modification are permitted—no other fields or whitespace in
the JSON may be altered.  
(Checking is automated, so be precise!)

The task is small: about three or four standard shell commands (mkdir, cp, sed,
echo, jq, etc.) should be all you need.
truth:
Initial files present before the agent starts:

1. Directory: /home/user/observability/templates  
2. File: /home/user/observability/templates/metrics_base.json  
   Contents (including exact spacing and newlines):

{
  "id": null,
  "title": "Base Metrics Dashboard",
  "uid": "base-metrics",
  "tags": [
    "base"
  ],
  "refresh": "10s",
  "panels": []
}

(There is a trailing newline after the closing brace.)

Verification data expected **after** the agent finishes:

A. /home/user/observability/dashboard/dashboard_config.json  
Exact contents (including spacing and newlines, ending with a trailing newline):

{
  "id": null,
  "title": "Prod API Overview",
  "uid": "base-metrics",
  "tags": [
    "base",
    "tuned"
  ],
  "refresh": "5s",
  "panels": []
}

B. /home/user/observability/tuning.log  
Exact contents (three lines, each terminated by \n and **nothing else**):

changed:title:Base Metrics Dashboard->Prod API Overview
changed:refresh:10s->5s
added:tag:tuned

Directory /home/user/observability/dashboard must have standard
user-writable permissions (0755 or 0775); same for the log file (0644).

The automated test will:
1. Verify that the dashboard_config.json file exists at the right path.
2. Compare its SHA-256 hash to the hash of the JSON block shown above.
3. Verify that tuning.log exists and its contents match exactly the three-line
   log shown above.
